texto: Such a shovel, it seemed a waste not to use it.
explicacao: Citação de Daniel Kraus sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Such a shovel, it seemed a waste not to use it.” articula reflexao, grave-robber, horror em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como horror e shovel.

texto: Darkness is a defining characteristic of Rotters. But it’s worthy to remember that darkness is just that—it’s dark—and what is being concealed in the dark is not just the horrible and fearsome, it’s also the inspirational and moving. Horror means nothing without happiness; dark means nothing without light. Rotters may make you feel scared, but hopefully it will also make you simply feel. It’s that kind of book, or at least I hope it is.

frase_original: Darkness is a defining characteristic of Rotters. But it’s worthy to remember that darkness is just that—it’s dark—and what is being concealed in the dark is not just the horrible and fearsome, it’s also the inspirational and moving. Horror means nothing without happiness; dark means nothing without light. Rotters may make you feel scared, but hopefully it will also make you simply feel. It’s that kind of book, or at least I hope it is.

texto: The weathered dairy barn, the wilted chicken coop, the leaning corn crib, the corroded silos-- all were revealed as structures of utility and grace. Someone must have rigged Ry's perception so that he had spent his whole life seeing only the ultimate futility of these structures while concealing what made them worthy, the struggle itself, the striving for a better day.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Daniel Kraus sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “The weathered dairy barn, the wilted chicken coop, the leaning corn crib, the corroded silos-- al…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a autoestima, life-lessons, living, útil em momentos de living.
